content.full_text:
Ravi Mehta
Chairman

Fair Political Practices Commission

November 9, 1995

Beverly Ross
Assistant Registrar of Voters
Tehama County
P.O. Box 250
Red Bluff, CA 96080

Re: Campaign Filing Schedule
Our File No. I-95-359

Dear Ms. Ross:

This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.1/ This letter constitutes written advice under
Section 84205, which states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required to
be filed by this title.

A recall election in Tehama County will be held on
February 6, 1996. In addition, the candidates involved in the
recall election will also be seeking election to the same office
on March 26, 1996.

The officeholders, candidates and committees involved in the
February 6, 1996 recall election and the March 26, 1996 election,
are advised to file campaign disclosure statements on the
following schedule:

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660

Beverly Ross
Our File No. I-95-359
Page 2


Type of Statement          Period Covered         Filing Deadline

Recall 1st Pre-Election   Jan. 1- Dec. 23       Dec. 28, 1995

Recall 2nd Pre-Election   Dec. 24-Jan. 20       Jan. 25, 1996

Recall Late Contribution;
  Late Independent
  Expenditure Reports     Jan. 21- Feb. 5       Within 24 hours

3-26 1st Pre-Election     Jan. 21-Feb. 10       Feb. 15, 1996

3-26 2nd Pre-Election     Feb. 11-Mar.  9       Mar. 14, 1996

3-26 Late Contribution;
  Late Independent
  Expenditure Reports     Mar. 10-Mar. 25       Within 24 hours

3-26 Semi-Annual          Mar. 10-June 30       July 31, 1996


Important Notes:

--     The period covered by any campaign statement begins on the
day after the closing date of the last statement filed. If no
previous statement has been filed during the calendar year, the
period covered by the first pre-election statement should begin
January 1, 1995.

--     Effective January 1, 1996, a candidate or committee that
makes a late "in-kind" contribution must notify the recipient of
the amount of the contribution within 24 hours. The recipient of
a late "in-kind" contribution must file a late contribution report
within 48 hours of the time the contribution was received.

--     Candidates and committees that will not be involved in the
March 26, 1996 election are not required to file the pre-election
statements due February 15th and March 14th. Instead they will
file a semi-annual statement in connection with the recall
election covering the period January 21 through June 30, 1996,
which is due by July 31, 1996.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Mary Ann Kammer
                                        Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: What is the appropriate campaign disclosure filing schedule for candidates and committees involved in both a February 6, 1996 recall election and a March 26, 1996 general election in Tehama County?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides a detailed filing schedule for recall election statements (with deadlines of December 28, 1995 and January 25, 1996) and March 26 election statements (with deadlines of February 15, March 14, and July 31, 1996), along with specific rules for late contributions and in-kind contributions effective January 1, 1996.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present; question and conclusion derived from document context and content. Document includes detailed filing schedule table and important notes section. Written advice provided under Government Code Section 84205.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the appropriate campaign disclosure filing schedule for candidates and committees involved in both a February 6, 1996 recall election and a March 26, 1996 general election in Tehama County?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides a detailed filing schedule for recall election statements (with deadlines of December 28, 1995 and January 25, 1996) and March 26 election statements (with deadlines of February 15, March 14, and July 31, 1996), along with specific rules for late contributions and in-kind contributions effective January 1, 1996.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter provides a campaign disclosure filing schedule for candidates and committees involved in a Tehama County recall election on February 6, 1996 and a concurrent general election on March 26, 1996, specifying periods covered, filing deadlines, and special rules for late contributions and in-kind contributions.
